tasks.title: Dynamic Array Summation
tasks.description:
In C programming, dynamic memory allocation allows programs to request memory from the operating system during runtime, rather than reserving a fixed amount at compile time. This is particularly useful when dealing with data structures like arrays whose sizes are not known in advance, such as when processing user input of varying lengths, enabling more flexible and efficient memory usage.

Your task is to write a C program that dynamically allocates memory for an array of integers, reads a specified number of integers into this array from standard input, calculates their sum, and then properly frees the allocated memory. The program must first read an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 1000), which represents the number of elements. Following `N`, it will read `N` individual integers (each between -10000 and 10000). Your program should compute the sum of these `N` integers and print the final sum to standard output, followed by a newline.

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: 3 10 20 30 → sum: 0 → 10 → 30 → 60. Output: 60.
